Avenues.description: Mexican/Mexican American criminal street gang based in Los Angeles County, California. Originally a social club founded by the Flores brothers in the 1940s, it evolved into a violent drug-trafficking organization. The gang is known for hate crimes against African-Americans and is under the control of the Mexican Mafia in prison. It remains active with an estimated 800 members.

Luppino crime family.description: The Luppino crime family is an 'Ndrangheta organized crime family based in Hamilton, Ontario, Canada, founded in the 1950s by Giacomo Luppino. It is one of three centralized Mafia organizations in Hamilton, with strong connections to the Buffalo crime family. By 2018, the Violi family had increased involvement, but both brothers were imprisoned for drug trafficking. The family is now believed to be the Buffalo family's Canadian crew.

Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai.description: The Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai is a yakuza criminal organization based on the Okinawa island of Japan. It was formed in 1990 when it split from the Kyokuryu-kai, led by Kiyoshi Tominaga. With an estimated 300 active members, it is the largest yakuza group in Okinawa Prefecture and a designated yakuza group under Japanese law. Its official policy forbids members from engaging in drug trafficking.

Whitney gang.description: The Whitney gang was a family-based criminal organization from the Anfield district of Liverpool, England, involved in drug trafficking. Active from the 1980s until 2011, the gang was led by members of the Whitney family. In November 2011, the family and their associates were sentenced to a total of 82 years in prison for conspiracy to supply Class A drugs. The gang's activities ended with the imprisonment of its members.

descriptions: The Colombo family is one of the Five Families.

In 1931, the Five Families were organized by Salvatore Maranzano following his victory in the Castellammarese War . Maranzano reorganized the Italian American gangs in New York City into the Maranzano, Profaci, Mangano, Luciano, and Gagliano families, which are now known as the Bonanno , Colombo , Gambino , Genovese , and Lucchese families, respectively.
